<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Monastic Archer Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5978) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 118

**Requirements**
You are unarmored and wielding a [longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=436), a [shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=437), or a bow with the [Monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656) trait.

</column>

---

You enter a specialized stance for a unique martial art centered around the use of a bow. While in this stance, the only Strikes you can make are those using longbows, shortbows, or bows with the monk trait. You can use [Flurry of Blows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2817) with these bows. You can use your other monk feats or monk abilities that normally require [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attacks with these bows when attacking within half the first range increment (normally 50 feet for a longbow and 30 feet for a shortbow), so long as the feat or ability doesn't require a single, specific Strike.

**Special** When you select this feat, you become trained in the longbow, shortbow, and any simple and martial bows with the monk trait. If you gain the expert strikes class feature, your proficiency rank for these weapons increases to expert, and if you gain the master strikes class feature, your proficiency rank for these weapons increases to master.

<title level="2">Monastic Archer Stance leads to...</title>
[Focused Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6026), [Return Fire](/Feats.aspx?ID=6004), [Triangle Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6047)
name: Monastic Archer Stance
requirement_markdown: You are unarmored and wielding a [longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=436), a [shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=437), or a bow with the [Monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656) trait.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Monastic Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5979) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 118

</column>

---

You have trained with the weaponry of your monastery or school. You gain access to [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=721) weapons with the [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656) trait and become trained in simple and martial monk weapons. When your proficiency for unarmed attacks increases to expert or master, your proficiency rank for these weapons increases to expert or master as well. If you have familiarity with an [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) or [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) weapon (such as from the [Catfolk Weapon Familiarity](/Feats.aspx?ID=5547) feat), that weapon also gains the monk trait for you.

 You can use melee monk weapons with any of your monk feats or monk abilities that normally require unarmed attacks, though not if the feat or ability requires you to use a single specific type of attack, such as [Crane Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=430). If you gain the [critical specialization benefit](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) for unarmed attacks—with the expert strikes class feature, for example— you also gain it with monk weapons.

<title level="2">Monastic Weaponry leads to...</title>
[Advanced Monastic Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5997), [Advanced Monastic Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=7108), [Shooting Stars Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5988), [Waterfowl Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7495), [Whirling Blade Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=6037)
name: Monastic Weaponry

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mountain Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5980) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 118

**Archetypes** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67) (Level 4), [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=255) (Level 4)

**Requirements**
You are unarmored and touching the ground.

</column>

---

You enter the stance of an implacable mountain—a technique created by [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) monks—allowing you to strike with the weight of an avalanche and block blows with your garments. The only Strikes you can make are falling stone unarmed attacks. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits.

 While in Mountain Stance, you gain a +4 item bonus to AC and a +2 circumstance bonus to any defenses against [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), and other [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) effects. You have a Dexterity modifier cap to your AC of +0, meaning you don't add your Dexterity to your AC, and your Speeds are all reduced by 5 feet. The item bonus to AC from Mountain Stance is cumulative with [_armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) runes on your [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39), [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), and [_bands of force_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3058).

<title level="2">Mountain Stance leads to...</title>
[Mountain Stronghold](/Feats.aspx?ID=6002)
name: Mountain Stance
requirement_markdown: You are unarmored and touching the ground.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stumbling Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5982) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 119

**Archetypes** [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=67) (Level 4), [Martial Artist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=255) (Level 4)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

You enter a seemingly unfocused stance that mimics the movements of the inebriated—bobbing, weaving, leaving false openings, and distracting your enemies from your true movements. While in this stance, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390). The only Strikes you can make are stumbling swing unarmed attacks. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. If an enemy hits you with a melee Strike while in this stance, it becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the next stumbling swing Strike you make against it before the end of your next turn.

<title level="2">Stumbling Stance leads to...</title>
[Stumbling Feint](/Feats.aspx?ID=6005)
name: Stumbling Stance

These deal 1d8 slashing damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. On a critical success with your tiger claws, if you deal damage, the target takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

 As long as your Speed is at least 20 feet while in Tiger Stance, you can Step 10 feet.

You are in Monastic Archer Stance and wielding a bow usable with that stance.

</column>

---

You string three arrows to your bow and fire them all at once. Make three ranged Strikes against a single target with the required weapon, each using your current multiple attack penalty, and you take an additional –2 penalty. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty, and you combine the attacks' damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. Your [Stunning Blows](/Feats.aspx?ID=5989) benefit applies to Triangle Shot, even though it isn't a [Flurry of Blows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2817). If all three Strikes hit, the target takes 3d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Godbreaker](/Feats.aspx?ID=6049) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 127

**Archetype** [Wrestler](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=270) (Level 20)

**Prerequisites**
[Crushing Grab](/Feats.aspx?ID=5985); [Whirling Throw](/Feats.aspx?ID=6008)

**Requirements**
You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90).

</column>

---

You have mastered an ultimate grappling technique capable of breaking even the mightiest of foes. You hurl the creature 20 feet into the air, following behind it with a powerful jump, and then make an unarmed Strike. If the Strike is successful, you can repeat this process up to two more times, moving the creature an additional 20 feet directly up into the air with each Strike; the creature keeps the grabbed or restrained condition throughout the sequence. If you miss with any of the Strikes, Godbreaker and the grabbed or restrained condition immediately end and both you and the opponent fall, taking [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) as normal for the total height of your jump.

 If all three Strikes are successful, you immediately grab the creature and bring it crashing to the ground, dealing your unarmed Strike damage plus falling damage to it. You land on your feet adjacent to the creature, you take no damage from the fall, and the creature remains grabbed or restrained by you.
name: Godbreaker

once per round

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 139 --- You open yourself to the guidance of whatever spirits or powers deign to help you. Roll 1d4 to see what type of spirit is drawn to you. Your next action must be the type of action the spirit prefers, but you also gain the listed benefit for the action as the spirit guides you. If you attempt to use a different action, you must succeed at a DC 6 flat check or the action is lost.
**1 Warrior** You must attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). You gain a +1 status bonus to your attack roll and a +2 status bonus to damage, or a +6 status bonus to damage if you are at least [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) 3.
**2 Adept** You must attempt a Perception check or a skill action. You gain a +1 status bonus to the check, or a +2 status bonus if you are cursebound 3.
**3 Sage** You must attempt to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734). You gain a status bonus to the spell's damage or healing equal to the spell's rank, or equal to the spell's rank + 3 if you are at least cursebound 3.
**4 Wanderer** You must attempt a [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) action, or a [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), or [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) action if you have the relevant speed. You gain a +10- foot status bonus to your Speed for the action, or a +20-foot status bonus if you are at least cursebound 3.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 140 **Archetype** [Exorcist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=171) (Level 8) --- You have a vague connection to the Ethereal Plane that enables you to notice spirits. While you're exploring but not [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630), the GM rolls a secret check for you to find haunts that usually require Searching, as well as spirits, creatures on the Ethereal Plane, and beings made entirely of spiritual essence such as [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), and [monitors](/Traits.aspx?ID=655).
You can also potentially notice [ethereal](/Traits.aspx?ID=593) creatures and spirits inside solid objects, provided they are within 30 feet of you. This applies while Searching, while [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), and on the automatic secret check you gain while exploring even if you aren't Searching. You can still notice spirits only on a successful check, and you can't see them if they're more than 5 feet inside an object. When you notice a creature with this sense, you also learn its location, making it [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to you if it had been [undetected.](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96)
name: Spiritual Sense

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 141 **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Prerequisites** [bones mystery](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=14) or [lore mystery](/Mysteries.aspx?ID=18) --- You roll a handful of bones to learn (or perhaps influence) the future course of events. Roll 1d4 and use the corresponding result below. Whenever you Roll the Bones of Fate, any effects from a previous usage immediately end.
**1 Good** You or an ally within 30 feet can roll twice on your next attack roll or skill check, taking the higher result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.
**2 Bad** One creature you are observing within 30 feet must succeed at a Will save against your spell DC; on a failure, the target must roll twice on their next attack roll or skill check that takes at least one action to perform, taking the lower result. This is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.
**3 Mixed** You gain the benefits of rolling both a 1 and a 2.
**4 Cursed Possibilities** Your attempts to meddle in the forces of prophecy bring dire consequences for all. Every creature within 30 feet of you when you perform the augury rolls twice on their next attack roll or skill check that takes at least one action to perform; if the highest number rolled is odd, they take the lower result, and if the highest number rolled is even, they take the higher result. If they took the lower result, this effect has the misfortune trait for them, and if they took the higher result, it has the fortune trait.
name: Roll the Bones of Fate

Select one revelation spell from a mystery other than your own. You can choose only an initial revelation spell or an advanced revelation spell.
This spell gains the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) trait for you, and when you cast it, you gain the cursebound 1 effects of its mystery in addition to your normal curse effects. Any ability that lets you use a cursebound ability without increasing the severity of your curse also prevents you from gaining this additional curse effect.
You can't cast your chosen revelation spell if any of the following conditions are true: its mystery's curse effects directly conflict with or negate the effects of your own mystery's curse, the curse would have no effect on you (for example, removing an ability from your original mystery that you lack), or if either of these criteria would be met once you finish [Casting the Spell.](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734)

The magic in your blood surges in response to your foe's spell. You generate a blood magic effect you know, even if you are already under the effects of blood magic. The target must be either you or the creature that triggered Blood Rising. If the blood magic effect grants you a bonus to AC or the appropriate saving throw, that bonus applies against the triggering spell. If the effect has a duration, it instead lasts until the beginning of your next turn.

**Special** This feat has the traitcorresponding to the tradition of your  [ bloodline](Bloodlines.aspx).

Attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367); you can use [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)instead of the skill normally needed for that subject.   • [**Primal**](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) The immense power of nature echoes in your voice. You can attempt a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) a target.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 157 **Prerequisites** a [bloodline](Bloodlines.aspx?ID=) based on a specific type of creature --- You permanently mutate to become more like the creatures of your bloodline. You gain the appropriate trait or traits for those types of creatures ([aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) for aberrant, [angel](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=359) and [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) for angelic, [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) and [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) for demonic, and so on). You gain low-light vision or darkvision, if one is appropriate for creatures with those traits. Choose one of the following.
  • If the creatures associated with your bloodline have the ability to [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), you gain a fly Speed equal to your land Speed.
  • If the creatures associated with your bloodline are [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533) or [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529), you become amphibious, able to breathe water and air equally well, and you gain a [swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) Speed equal to your land Speed.
  • If creatures associated with your bloodline have a resistance or immunity to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697),choose an energy type your bloodline is immune or resistant to and gain resistance 20 against that energy type.
name: Bloodline Mutation
prerequisite_markdown: a [bloodline](Bloodlines.aspx?ID=) based on a specific type of creature

You toss one weapon into the air as a distraction as you throw another. Choose a creature within 30 feet and [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) against it; you can attempt this Feint even if the target is not within melee range, and if the target becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) as a result of the Feint, this applies to your [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) weapon attacks for the duration. If you have an open hand, you then catch the weapon you originally tossed into the air unless you critically failed at your attempt to Feint, in which case it lands on the ground at your feet.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 167 --- Your attack harms and hinders your foe. Choose a particular part of your foe from the list below and Strike. If you hit and damage the target, apply the effect corresponding to the chosen part. This hindrance lasts until the end of your next turn. On a critical hit, you also apply a lesser effect lasting for 1 minute.
  • **Arm** (or another limb used for attacks, such as a tentacle) The target is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71). On a critical hit, it is also enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
  • **Head** The target is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94). On a critical hit, it is also stupefied 1 for 1 minute.
  • **Legs** The target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds. On a critical hit, it also takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute.
name: Targeting Finisher

Attempt to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) a creature at least one size category larger than you, using the following effects. **Critical Success** You move through the enemy's space, treating the squares in its space as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) (every 5 feet costs 10 feet of movement). If you don't have enough Speed to move all the way through its space, you get the same effect as a failure. If you successfully pass through the target's square, the target gains weakness to your precision damage equal to half your level, which lasts until the end of your turn.
**Success** As success, but the weakness applies only against the next attack you make against the target.
**Failure** Your movement ends, and you trigger reactions as if you had moved out of the square you started in.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and you fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: The Bigger They Are

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=825" />
</traits>

**Requirements**

</column>

---

You stylishly leap and deliver a powerful finisher. Make a [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) and attempt one single-action finisher at any point during your jump; the finisher can't be one that includes other movement, such as Mobile Finisher. Immediately after the finisher, you fall to the ground if you're in the air, even if you haven't reached the maximum distance of your jump. If the distance you fall is no more than the height of your jump, you take no damage and land upright. When attempting a High Jump or Long Jump during a Flamboyant Leap, determine the DC using the Long Jump DCs, and increase the maximum distance to double your Speed, rather than just your Speed.
name: Flamboyant Leap

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 169

**Prerequisites**
Opportune Riposte

</column>

---

Your ripostes can deflect attacks back at their source. You can use Opportune Riposte with a trigger of “A foe outside of your reach critically fails an attack roll against you” in addition to its usual trigger. When you use Opportune Riposte with this new trigger against a ranged attack, your Strike deflects some of the triggering effect back toward its source. Compare the result of your attack roll to the AC of the triggering foe. On a hit, you deal the normal amount of damage for your Strike, but the damage type changes to that of the triggering attack. For instance, if you used Opportune Riposte to deflect a [_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), your Strike would deal fire damage instead of its normal damage type.
name: Impossible Riposte
